name: Aerospace Engineering
overview: Design aircraft, rockets and satellites. Aerospace Engineering sits in the Advanced Engineering family of Engineering & Manufacturing. It is a broad career direction — you can enter it through several routes and specialize later without restarting.
who_is_this_for: Numbers and patterns make sense to you
You keep asking why until you reach the bottom of things
who_should_avoid: You want a short, easy study path — this one demands years of serious preparation
Intense competition drains you — entry here is a contest
You want a laptop-anywhere lifestyle — this work happens in person
daily_responsibilities: Collaborate with your team using propulsion
Keep improving at structures — the field keeps moving
Communicate progress, results and decisions to the people who depend on them
work_environment: Primarily on-site — offices, facilities or the field depending on the role
scope: Demand outlook in India is strong; see FAQs for how the platform keeps this current.
advantages: A broad Advanced Engineering foundation that opens multiple specializations later
challenges: Fierce competition for the best seats and roles
A long, demanding preparation and learning curve
Location-bound work; postings and relocation are part of the deal
Early years demand patience before the rewards compound
misconceptions: “You must pick your exact job title now.” Aerospace Engineering is a direction — specialization comes later.
“Only toppers make it.” Consistent skill-building beats one exam rank in the long run.
portfolio_ideas: A documented case study of propulsion
projects: Beginner: explore aerodynamics with a small self-chosen project
Intermediate: build or run something real using MATLAB
smaller_city_scope: Concentrated in bigger hubs today; smaller-city presence is growing slowly.
rural_scope: Limited rural presence; expect to base yourself in a town or city.
home_state_note: Ask the AI coach about your state — regional demand, cities and relocation advice are generated live from retrieved data, never hardcoded.

Toward Defence Research & Development: build the shared skills and take a bridging project or course
faqs: What does Aerospace Engineering pay in India?
Entry roles typically start around ₹6 LPA and senior professionals reach ₹25 LPA or more. Live, region-specific salary data is retrieved by the platform when you ask.
What should I study after Class 12?
Focus on Physics, Mathematics. Typical degrees: B.Tech Aerospace/Aeronautical. Alternatives exist: diploma + lateral entry to b.tech.
Will AI take over this career?
AI impact is minimal — deeply human/physical work. Broad paths like Aerospace Engineering evolve rather than disappear — the students who pair domain skill with AI tools benefit most.
Is this a broad path or a specific job?
Aerospace Engineering is a career direction. Once inside it you can specialize further — the platform will map specializations in future versions.
